Title: New Low Quality option in the Hide comment menu
Author: Allison
EnhancedContent:
GitHub moderators can now classify hidden comments as **Low Quality**, a new option in the **Hide comment** dropdown menu available across issues, discussions, pull requests, and commits.

Previously, the available classification options (e.g., **Spam** and **Abuse**) were designed around traditionally problematic content and didn’t accurately reflect the growing volume of comments that are unhelpful or otherwise low quality. The new **Low Quality** classifier gives moderators a more precise tool to accurately categorize these comments during moderation.

![Screenshot showing the Hide comment dropdown menu with the new Low Quality option selected](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d1ea20bc-92c0-41f1-8761-f782e93d8b0b)

## How to use it

1. Navigate to any comment on an issue, discussion, pull request, or commit.
2. Select the **…** menu on the comment.
3. Choose **Hide comment**.
4. Select **Low Quality** from the classification dropdown.

Drop your feedback in our [Community discussion](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/191826).
